tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mildly elevated HbA1c, indicating impaired glucose metabolism or possible prediabetes, despite a normal fasting glucose. Chloride is slightly elevated, which may reflect mild dehydration or metabolic changes but is not clinically significant in isolation. Renal function, electrolytes, and other parameters are within normal limits. Recommend monitoring glucose control and clinical correlation for chloride.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus

text.div: Provenance for SYNDERAI Synthetic Example Data Resource
Laboratory: Laboratoire Central Européenne, 1000 Brussels, Belgium
